held_out_queries: What gets a paper rejected before peer review at AJS (an op-ed-style current-event essay with no new data)?
Who decides which reviewers see my AJS paper on first submission, given it's double-blind?
Does AJS avoid sending my paper to people in my network?

edits.summary: Add the AJS 'preject' (desk-reject) filter (papers not in dialogue with current sociology, opinion pieces, or current-event interpretations without original research), the editorship/assignment structure (three levels; UChicago Sociology faculty board; Manuscript Assignment Board of Assistant Editors supervised by graduate-student Associate Editors), and network-based reviewer avoidance + non-disclosure on the double-blind line. Fee amount kept live-check.
edits.gate_reason: strict improvement 56 -> 100; judge confirmed zero wrong facts; decision timeline / acceptance rate / APC absent from source map, correctly not invented
